title: ISE PSN Authentication Distribution Imbalance and Sticky-Session Detection
dataSources: ISE syslog *Passed-Authentications* (5200) — count auths per PSN. Authentication is normally distributed across PSNs by NAD primary/secondary RADIUS server config or by load-balancer (F5, Catalyst PBR-LB).
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=5200
| stats count as auth_count by host
| eventstats avg(auth_count) as avg_count, stdev(auth_count) as stdev_count
| eval z_score=(auth_count - avg_count) / stdev_count
| where abs(z_score) > 2
description: Detects when one PSN is receiving disproportionately more or fewer auths than its peers — usually caused by NAD misconfiguration (forgotten secondary RADIUS), load-balancer health-check failure, or sticky-session affinity gone wrong.
value: Imbalanced PSN load means one PSN works hard while others sit idle — and is often the first sign of a load-balancer or RADIUS-server-list misconfiguration. Catching it preserves capacity headroom on the busy PSN.
visualization: Per-PSN auth distribution; z-score chart; load-balancer health.
knownFalsePositives:
(a) Geographic load distribution — campus PSNs serve only local NADs.
(b) Active/standby designs.
mitreAttack: N/A (SLO monitoring — measures PSN load distribution, no adversary technique)

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.7 ICT risk — load distribution.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Disable secondary RADIUS server on a NAD; one PSN receives all auth traffic. UC-17.1.78 fires.

grandmaExplanation: ISE has multiple servers. They should share work equally. We notice when one server suddenly does much more (or less) than the others, which usually means something is misconfigured.
